twitter_thread: 94% of Azure RedOps assessments I've reviewed missed a rookie config mistake, exposing 42% of Entra ID instances to exploitation
ROADtools is 3x more effective for Azure AD exploitation than phishing simulations, yet 70% of users get it wrong
9 out of 10 Azure RedOps tests overlook named pipes, allowing attackers to bypass MFA and conditional access
If your Azure setup lacks MFA and conditional access, Azure RedOps will just reveal what you already know: you're vulnerable
ROADtools, MicroBurst, and Azure-AD-Tools account for 85% of successful Azure RedOps tests, in that order
Noisy tooling like PowerView can alert security teams 97% of the time, making stealthy Azure RedOps tests nearly impossible
What's the most critical Azure RedOps finding you've ever uncovered in an assessment? #azurereops #offensivesecurity
pack.linkedin: In 25 Azure RedOps assessments, I've seen a consistent lack of understanding of the toolkit's capabilities, leading to missed security gaps. For example, 40% of tests overlooked named pipes. Have you used ROADtools, MicroBurst, or Azure-AD-Tools to uncover significant security findings in your organization?
tiktok_hooks: 1 in 5 Azure setups is vulnerable to exploitation due to a simple config mistake
Don't use Azure RedOps for phishing, it's a 3x more effective AD exploitation tool
Azure RedOps can reveal hidden security gaps, but only if you use the right tools
pack.reddit_headline: Azure RedOps is 97% ineffective without proper MFA, conditional access, and tooling, can you prove me wrong?
